content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but RunSessionManager.swift imports CoreLocation, requests `requestWhenInUseAuthorization()`, calls `startUpdatingLocation()`, and stores GPS route data in `route: [CLLocation]` (App/RunSessionManager.swift:1-14); the app description also advertises "maps every run with GPS."
Pierre: Apple cares deeply about privacy policies being truthful since they're a core part of user trust and App Privacy label verification — reviewers actively cross-check policy text against evident app functionality. Here the policy directly contradicts both the code (active CoreLocation tracking) and the marketing copy (GPS mapping), which is exactly the kind of direct contradiction that triggers rejection. Fix by updating the privacy policy to accurately disclose location collection, storage, and any sync/iCloud handling of that data.
